label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking about the passage where they talk about the steps taken when looking for a missing child. The passage starts with putting an APB (which I think is an All-Points Bulletin) to all police stations. Then they call hospitals because the person might have been in an accident. Then they try parents, friends, or relatives. They follow the person's movements and check the last person they saw, looking in local or national papers they might read. Other methods include putting posters, going on TV. In America, there's a magazine with photos of missing children. But it says that with nearly two million missing children every year, they can't do all these things for everyone because of time, money, and lack of workers. The question is asking according to the passage, when looking for a missing child, what do they do. The options are: A) can always find him B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above C) just try one way or another D) won't give up until they find him Looking at the passage, it says they can't do all these things for everyone. So the correct answer would be that they couldn't always try every way mentioned. That's option B. Let me check again. The passage says "we can't do all these things for everyone. We haven't got the time, or the money, or the people who work for it." So that supports option B. The other options don't match. A is incorrect because the passage doesn't say they can always find the child. C is not directly stated; the passage lists multiple methods but says they can't do all of
